mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month. Higher policy rates signal tighter financial conditions that reduce corporate profit margins and equity demand.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with rate increases in >65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or trigger condition fails to predict direction with accuracy >55% over walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and personal savings indicates robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet strength, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during expansion phases, with 5-day forward returns showing no significant positive correlation (p > 0.10) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation triggered instances, or CPIAUCSL threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative predictive accuracy (<50%) for 60-day returns, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased residential construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, which stimulates related sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST series shows no significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window), or positive returns occur in <50% of high-housing-start periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with elevated federal funds rates indicates a supply-side contraction under tight monetary policy, which suppresses corporate output and earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where both proxies signal contraction, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series baselines in walk-forward validation.
